sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: This visit with a FTF encounter with the patient in the home is not a visit of convenience. Patient is seen in the home because there is a medical necessity for this visit to be conducted in the patient's environment. It is medically necessary and safer for the patient to be seen in the patient's own environment today.

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is homebound due to medical necessity and safety concerns.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires home health services due to medical necessity and safety concerns.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is an 87-year-old male who presented for follow-up of pneumonia, reporting significant improvement in symptoms. He has experienced decreased appetite and weight loss since completing his antibiotic course one week ago. He also reports lower back tenderness after hitting something in his bedroom. The patient's vitals are stable, and his X-ray shows improvement with resolution of pneumonia. The plan includes adding Ensure protein shakes between meals, prescribing an appetite stimulant, and monitoring appetite and weight improvement.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: PT/OT to evaluate and treat appropriately to increase functionality, decreases debility and morbidity. Increase strength, balance, and cardio/pulmonary function. Improve ability to perform ADL's safely and independently. Patient is to improve and/or return to baseline.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: The plan for the next visit includes monitoring appetite and weight improvement, and contacting the office if appetite does not improve for possible additional medication.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The care plan includes monitoring for disease progression, evaluating patient's knowledge deficit and teaching accordingly, and providing skilled nursing for teachings of disease process, diet, and new medication regimen.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: The patient and caregiver goals include improving appetite and weight, managing lower back tenderness, and preventing further weight loss.
